signature: squidpy.datasets.visium_hne_image(path=None, **kwargs)
description: H&E image from 10x Genomics Visium dataset. The shape of this image is (11757, 11291).

params.name: path
params.desc: Path where to save the .tiff image.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for squidpy.im.ImageContainer.add_img()

misc: Returns an instance of squidpy.im.ImageContainer which contains the image data.

signature: squidpy.datasets.slideseqv2(path=None, **kwargs)
description: Pre-processed SlideseqV2 dataset from Stickles et al. The shape of this anndata.AnnData object is (41786, 4000).

params.name: path
params.desc: Path where to save the dataset.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for scanpy.read().

params.name: return
params.desc: The dataset.

signature: squidpy.pl.ligrec(adata, cluster_key=None, source_groups=None, target_groups=None, means_range=(-inf, inf), pvalue_threshold=1.0, remove_empty_interactions=True, remove_nonsig_interactions=False, dendrogram=None, alpha=0.001, swap_axes=False, title=None, figsize=None, dpi=None, save=None, **kwargs)
description: Plot the result of a receptor-ligand permutation test.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object. It can also be a dict, as returned by squidpy.gr.ligrec().

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obs where clustering is stored. Only used when adata is of type AnnData.

params.name: source_groups
params.desc: Source interaction clusters. If None, select all clusters.

params.name: target_groups
params.desc: Target interaction clusters. If None, select all clusters.

params.name: means_range
params.desc: Only show interactions whose means are within this closed interval.

params.name: pvalue_threshold
params.desc: Only show interactions with p-value <= pvalue_threshold.

params.name: remove_empty_interactions
params.desc: Remove rows and columns that only contain interactions with NaN values.

params.name: remove_nonsig_interactions
params.desc: Remove rows and columns that only contain interactions that are larger than alpha.

params.name: dendrogram
params.desc: How to cluster based on the p-values. Valid options are: None - do not perform clustering., 'interacting_molecules' - cluster the interacting molecules., 'interacting_clusters' - cluster the interacting clusters., 'both' - cluster both rows and columns. Note that in this case, the dendrogram is not shown.

params.name: alpha
params.desc: Significance threshold. All elements with p-values <= alpha will be marked by tori instead of dots.

params.name: swap_axes
params.desc: Whether to show the cluster combinations as rows and the interacting pairs as columns.

params.name: title
params.desc: Title of the plot.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: dpi
params.desc: Dots per inch.

params.name: save
params.desc: Whether to save the plot.

params.name: scalebar_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for matplotlib_scalebar.ScaleBar().

params.name: edges_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for networkx.draw_networkx_edges().

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for matplotlib.pyplot.scatter() or matplotlib.pyplot.imshow().

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for scanpy.pl.DotPlot.style() or scanpy.pl.DotPlot.legend().

params.name: return
params.desc: Nothing, just plots the figure and optionally saves the plot.

misc: $molecule_1$ belongs to the source clusters displayed on the top (or on the right, if `swap_axes = True`, whereas $molecule_2$ belongs to the target clusters.

signature: squidpy.gr.mask_graph(sdata, table_key, polygon_mask, negative_mask=False, spatial_key='spatial', key_added='mask', copy=False)
description: Mask the graph based on a polygon mask.

params.name: sdata
params.desc: The spatial data object.

params.name: table_key
params.desc: The key of the table containing the spatial data.

params.name: polygon_mask
params.desc: The shapely.Polygon or shapely.MultiPolygon to be used as mask.

params.name: negative_mask
params.desc: Whether to keep the edges within the polygon mask or outside. Note that when `negative_mask = True`, only the edges fully contained in the polygon are removed. If edges are partially contained in the polygon, they are kept.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsm where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key which controls where the results are saved if `copy = False`.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the `adata` object.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `copy = True`, returns a tuple with the masked spatial connectivities and masked distances matrices. Otherwise, modifies the `adata` with the following keys: - anndata.AnnData.obsp ['{{key_added}}_{{spatial_key}}_connectivities'] - the spatial connectivities. - anndata.AnnData.obsp ['{{key_added}}_{{spatial_key}}_distances'] - the spatial distances. - anndata.AnnData.uns ['{{key_added}}_{{spatial_key}}'] - dict containing parameters.

misc: The polygon_mask must be in the same coordinate_systems of the spatial graph, but no check is performed to assess this.

signature: squidpy.gr.centrality_scores(adata, cluster_key, score=None, connectivity_key=None, copy=False, n_jobs=None, backend='loky', show_progress_bar=False)
description: Compute centrality scores per cluster or cell type.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: score
params.desc: Centrality measures as described in `networkx.algorithms.centrality`. If None, use all the options below.

params.name: connectivity_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obsp` where spatial connectivities are stored. Default is `['spatial_connectivities']`.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the `adata` object.

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs.

params.name: backend
params.desc: Parallelization backend to use.

params.name: show_progress_bar
params.desc: Whether to show the progress bar or not.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `copy = True`, returns a pandas DataFrame. Otherwise, modifies the `adata`.

misc: Inspired by usage in Gene Regulatory Networks (GRNs) in Kamimoto et al., 2020.

signature: squidpy.datasets.sc_mouse_cortex(path=None, **kwargs)
description: Pre-processed scRNA-seq mouse cortex dataset.

params.name: path
params.desc: Path where to save the dataset.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for scanpy.read().

params.name: return
params.desc: The dataset.

misc: The shape of the anndata.AnnData object is (21697, 36826).

signature: squidpy.tl.var_by_distance(adata, groups, cluster_key=None, library_key=None, library_id=None, design_matrix_key='design_matrix', covariates=None, metric='euclidean', spatial_key='spatial', copy=False)
description: Build a design matrix consisting of distance measurements to selected anchor point(s) for each observation.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: groups
params.desc: Anchor point(s) to calculate distances to. Can be a single or multiple observations as long as they are annotated in an .obs column with name given by cluster_key. Alternatively, a numpy array of coordinates can be passed.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Name of annotation column in .obs where the observation used as anchor points are located.

params.name: library_key
params.desc: If multiple library_id, column in anndata.AnnData.obs which stores mapping between library_id and obs.

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Name of the Z-dimension(s) that this function should be applied to. For not specified Z-dimensions, the identity function is applied.

params.name: design_matrix_key
params.desc: Name of the design matrix saved to .obsm.

params.name: covariates
params.desc: Additional covariates from .obs to include in the design matrix.

params.name: metric
params.desc: Distance metric, defaults to 'euclidean'.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsm where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If True, return the result, otherwise save it to the adata object.

params.name: return
params.desc: If copy = True, returns the design_matrix with the distances to an anchor point. Otherwise, stores design_matrix in .obsm.

signature: squidpy.datasets.four_i(path=None, **kwargs)
description: Pre-processed subset 4i dataset from Gut et al. The shape of this anndata.AnnData object is (270876, 43).

params.name: path
params.desc: Path where to save the dataset.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for scanpy.read().

params.name: return
params.desc: The dataset.

signature: squidpy.pl.spatial_segment(adata, color=None, groups=None, library_id=None, library_key=None, spatial_key='spatial', img=True, img_res_key='hires', img_alpha=None, img_cmap=None, img_channel=None, seg=None, seg_key='segmentation', seg_cell_id=None, seg_contourpx=None, seg_outline=False, use_raw=None, layer=None, alt_var=None, crop_coord=None, cmap=None, palette=None, alpha=1.0, norm=None, na_color=(0, 0, 0, 0), connectivity_key=None, edges_width=1.0, edges_color='grey', library_first=True, frameon=None, wspace=None, hspace=0.25, ncols=4, outline=False, outline_color=('black', 'white'), outline_width=(0.3, 0.05), legend_loc='right margin', legend_fontsize=None, legend_fontweight='bold', legend_fontoutline=None, legend_na=True, colorbar=True, scalebar_dx=None, scalebar_units=None, title=None, axis_label=None, fig=None, ax=None, return_ax=False, figsize=None, dpi=None, save=None, scalebar_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), edges_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), **kwargs)
description: Plot spatial omics data with segmentation masks on top.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: color
params.desc: Key for annotations in `anndata.AnnData.obs` or variables/genes.

params.name: groups
params.desc: For discrete annotation in `color`, select which values to plot (other values are set to NAs).

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Name of the Z-dimension(s) that this function should be applied to. For not specified Z-dimensions, the identity function is applied.

params.name: library_key
params.desc: If multiple library_id, column in `anndata.AnnData.obs` which stores mapping between `library_id` and obs.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obsm` where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: img
params.type: bool | ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[Any]] | Sequence[ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[Any]]] | None
params.desc: Whether to plot the image. One (or more) `numpy.ndarray` can also be passed for plotting.

params.name: img_res_key
params.desc: Key for image resolution, used to get `img` and `scale_factor` from `'images'` and `'scalefactors'` entries for this library.

params.name: img_alpha
params.desc: Alpha value for the underlying image.

params.name: img_cmap
params.desc: Colormap for the image, see `matplotlib.colors.Colormap`.

params.name: img_channel
params.desc: To select which channel to plot (all by default).

params.name: seg
params.type: bool | ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[Any]] | Sequence[ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[Any]]] | None
params.desc: Whether to plot the segmentation mask. One (or more) `numpy.ndarray` can also be passed for plotting.

params.name: seg_key
params.desc: Key of segmentation mask in `anndata.AnnData.uns`.

params.name: seg_cell_id
params.desc: Column in `anndata.AnnData.obs` with unique segmentation mask ids. Required to filter valid segmentation masks.

params.name: seg_contourpx
params.desc: Draw contour of specified width for each segment. If None, fills entire segment.

params.name: seg_outline
params.desc: Whether to plot boundaries around segmentation masks.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: If True, use `anndata.AnnData.raw`.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.layers` or None for `anndata.AnnData.X`.

params.name: alt_var
params.desc: Which column to use in `anndata.AnnData.var` to select alternative `var_name`.

params.name: crop_coord
params.type: tuple[int, int, int, int] | Sequence[tuple[int, int, int, int]] | None
params.desc: Coordinates to use for cropping the image (left, right, top, bottom). These coordinates are expected to be in pixel space (same as `spatial`) and will be transformed by `scale_factor`. If not provided, image is automatically cropped to bounds of `spatial`, plus a border.

params.name: cmap
params.desc: Colormap for continuous annotations, see `matplotlib.colors.Colormap`.

params.name: palette
params.desc: Palette for discrete annotations, see `matplotlib.colors.Colormap`.

params.name: alpha
params.desc: Alpha value for scatter point/shape.

params.name: norm
params.desc: Colormap normalization for continuous annotations, see `matplotlib.colors.Normalize`.

params.name: na_color
params.desc: Color to be used for NAs values, if present.

params.name: connectivity_key
params.desc: Key for neighbors graph to plot. Default is: `anndata.AnnData.obsp` `['spatial_connectivities']`.

params.name: edges_width
params.desc: Width of the edges. Only used when `connectivity_key != None` .

params.name: edges_color
params.desc: Color of the edges.

params.name: library_first
params.desc: If multiple libraries are plotted, set the plotting order with respect to `color`.

params.name: frameon
params.desc: If True, draw a frame around the panels.

params.name: wspace
params.desc: Width space between panels.

params.name: hspace
params.desc: Height space between panels.

params.name: ncols
params.desc: Number of panels per row.

params.name: outline
params.desc: If True, a thin border around points/shapes is plotted.

params.name: outline_color
params.desc: Color of the border.

params.name: outline_width
params.desc: Width of the border.

params.name: legend_loc
params.desc: Location of the legend, see `matplotlib.legend.Legend`.

params.name: legend_fontsize
params.desc: Font size of the legend, see `matplotlib.text.Text.set_fontsize()`.

params.name: legend_fontweight
params.desc: Font weight of the legend, see `matplotlib.text.Text.set_fontweight()`.

params.name: legend_fontoutline
params.desc: Font outline of the legend, see `matplotlib.patheffects.withStroke`.

params.name: legend_na
params.desc: Whether to show NA values in the legend.

params.name: colorbar
params.desc: Whether to show the colorbar, see `matplotlib.pyplot.colorbar()`.

params.name: scalebar_dx
params.desc: Size of one pixel in units specified by `scalebar_units`.

params.name: scalebar_units
params.desc: Units of `scalebar_dx`.

params.name: title
params.desc: Panel titles.

params.name: axis_label
params.desc: Panel axis labels.

params.name: fig
params.desc: Optional `matplotlib.figure.Figure` to use.

params.name: ax
params.desc: Optional `matplotlib.axes.Axes` to use.

params.name: return_ax
params.desc: Whether to return `matplotlib.axes.Axes` object(s).

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: dpi
params.desc: Dots per inch.

params.name: save
params.desc: Whether to save the plot.

params.name: scalebar_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib_scalebar.ScaleBar()`.

params.name: edges_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `networkx.draw_networkx_edges()`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.pyplot.scatter()` or `matplotlib.pyplot.imshow()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Nothing, just plots the figure and optionally saves the plot.

misc: SEE ALSO - `squidpy.pl.spatial_scatter()` on how to plot spatial data with overlayed data on top.

signature: squidpy.datasets.visium_hne_image_crop(path=None, **kwargs)
description:
Cropped H&E image from 10x Genomics Visium dataset.
The shape of this image is (3527, 3527).

params.name: path
params.desc: Path where to save the .tiff image.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for squidpy.im.ImageContainer.add_img().

params.name: return
params.desc: The image data.

signature: squidpy.im.segment(img, layer=None, library_id=None, method='watershed', channel=0, chunks=None, lazy=False, layer_added=None, copy=False, **kwargs)
description: Segment an image.

params.name: img
params.desc: High-resolution image.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Image layer in `img` that should be processed. If None and only 1 layer is present, it will be selected.

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Name of the Z-dimension(s) that this function should be applied to. For not specified Z-dimensions, the identity function is applied. If None, all Z-dimensions are segmented separately.

params.name: method
params.desc: Segmentation method to use. Valid options are: 'watershed'. Alternatively, any callable can be passed as long as it has the following signature: `numpy.ndarray (height, width, channels) -> numpy.ndarray (height, width[, channels])`.

params.name: channel
params.desc: Channel index to use for segmentation. If None, use all channels.

params.name: chunks
params.desc: Number of chunks for `dask`. For automatic chunking, use `chunks = 'auto'`.

params.name: lazy
params.desc: Whether to lazily compute the result or not. Only used when `chunks != None`.

params.name: layer_added
params.desc: Layer of new image layer to add into `img` object. If None, use `'segmented_{model}'`.

params.name: thresh
params.desc: Threshold for creation of masked image. The areas to segment should be contained in this mask. If None, it is determined by Otsu’s method. Only used if `method = 'watershed'`.

params.name: geq
params.desc: Treat `thresh` as upper or lower bound for defining areas to segment. If `geq = True`, mask is defined as `mask = arr >= thresh`, meaning high values in `arr` denote areas to segment. Only used if `method = 'watershed'`.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the image container.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for the underlying model.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `copy = True`, returns a new container with the segmented image in `'{layer_added}'`. Otherwise, modifies the `img` with the following key: `squidpy.im.ImageContainer['{layer_added}']` - the segmented image.

signature: squidpy.pl.ripley(adata, cluster_key, mode='F', plot_sims=True, palette=None, figsize=None, dpi=None, save=None, ax=None, legend_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), **kwargs)
description: Plot Ripley’s statistics for each cluster.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: mode
params.desc: Ripley’s statistics to be plotted.

params.name: plot_sims
params.desc: Whether to overlay simulations in the plot.

params.name: palette
params.desc: Categorical colormap for the clusters. If `None`, use `anndata.AnnData.uns['{cluster_key}_colors']`, if available.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: dpi
params.desc: Dots per inch.

params.name: save
params.desc: Whether to save the plot.

params.name: scalebar_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib_scalebar.ScaleBar()`.

params.name: edges_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `networkx.draw_networkx_edges()`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.pyplot.scatter()` or `matplotlib.pyplot.imshow()`.

params.name: ax
params.desc: Axes, `matplotlib.axes.Axes`.

params.name: legend_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.pyplot.legend()`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `seaborn.lineplot()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Nothing, just plots the figure and optionally saves the plot.

misc: The estimate is computed by `squidpy.gr.ripley()`. The function draws Ripley's statistics for specified clusters within spatial transcriptomics data.

signature: squidpy.read.vizgen(path, *, counts_file, meta_file, transformation_file=None, library_id='library', **kwargs)
description: Read *Vizgen* formatted dataset. In addition to reading the regular *Vizgen* output, it loads the metadata file and optionally loads the transformation matrix.

params.name: path
params.desc: Path to the root directory containing *Vizgen* files.

params.name: counts_file
params.desc: File containing the counts. Typically ends with *\_cell_by_gene.csv*.

params.name: meta_file
params.desc: File containing the spatial coordinates and additional cell-level metadata.

params.name: transformation_file
params.desc: Transformation matrix file for converting micron coordinates into pixels in images.

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Identifier for the *Vizgen* library. Useful when concatenating multiple `anndata.AnnData` objects.

params.name: return
params.desc:
Annotated data object with the following keys: 
- `anndata.AnnData.obsm['spatial']` - spatial spot coordinates in microns.
- `anndata.AnnData.obsm['blank_genes']` - blank genes from Vizgen platform.
- `anndata.AnnData.uns['spatial']['{library_id}']['scalefactors']['transformation_matrix']` - transformation matrix for converting micron coordinates to pixels. Only present if `transformation_file != None`.

misc:
SEE ALSO
- Vizgen data release program.
- `squidpy.pl.spatial_scatter()` on how to plot spatial data.

signature: squidpy.im.calculate_image_features(adata, img, layer=None, library_id=None, features='summary', features_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), key_added='img_features', copy=False, n_jobs=None, backend='loky', show_progress_bar=True, **kwargs)
description: Calculate image features for all observations in 'adata'.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: img
params.desc: High-resolution image.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Image layer in 'img' that should be processed. If None and only 1 layer is present, it will be selected.

params.name: library_id
params.desc: If None, there should only be one entry in anndata.AnnData.uns '['{spatial_key}']'. If a str, first search anndata.AnnData.obs '['{library_id}']' which contains the mapping from observations to library ids, then search anndata.AnnData.uns '['{spatial_key}'].

params.name: features
params.desc: Features to be calculated, like 'texture', 'summary', 'histogram', 'segmentation', 'custom'.

params.name: features_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for the different features that should be generated, such as '{ 'texture': { ... }, ... }'.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsm where to store the calculated features.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If 'True', return the result, otherwise save it to the 'adata' object.

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs.

params.name: backend
params.desc: Parallelization backend to use. See joblib.Parallel for available options.

params.name: show_progress_bar
params.desc: Whether to show the progress bar or not.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for squidpy.im.ImageContainer.generate_spot_crops().

params.name: return
params.desc: If 'copy = True', returns a pandas.DataFrame where columns correspond to the calculated features. Otherwise, modifies the 'adata' object with the following key: anndata.AnnData.uns '['{key_added}']' - the above mentioned dataframe.

misc: Raises: ValueError – If a feature is not known.

signature: squidpy.pl.var_by_distance(adata, var, anchor_key, design_matrix_key='design_matrix', stack_vars=False, covariate=None, order=5, show_scatter=True, color=None, line_palette=None, scatter_palette='viridis', dpi=None, figsize=None, save=None, title=None, axis_label=None, return_ax=None, regplot_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), scatterplot_kwargs=mappingproxy({}))
description: Plot a variable using a smooth regression line with increasing distance to an anchor point.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: var
params.desc: Variables to plot on y-axis.

params.name: anchor_key
params.desc: Anchor point column from which distances are taken.

params.name: design_matrix_key
params.desc: Name of the design matrix, previously computed with `squidpy.tl.var_by_distance()`, to use.

params.name: stack_vars
params.desc: Whether to show multiple variables on the same plot. Only works if ‘covariate’ is not specified.

params.name: covariate
params.desc: A covariate for which separate regression lines are plotted for each category.

params.name: order
params.desc: Order of the polynomial fit for `seaborn.regplot()`.

params.name: show_scatter
params.desc: Whether to show a scatter plot underlying the regression line.

params.name: color
params.desc: Variables in adata.obs to plot if ‘show_scatter==True’.

params.name: line_palette
params.desc: Categorical color palette used in case a covariate is specified.

params.name: scatter_palette
params.desc: Color palette for the scatter plot underlying the `seaborn.regplot()`.

params.name: dpi
params.desc: Dots per inch.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: save
params.desc: Whether to save the plot.

params.name: title
params.desc: Panel titles.

params.name: axis_label
params.desc: Panel axis labels.

params.name: return_ax
params.desc: Whether to return `matplotlib.axes.Axes` object(s).

params.name: regplot_kwargs
params.desc: Kwargs for `seaborn.regplot()`.

params.name: scatterplot_kwargs
params.desc: Kwargs for `matplotlib.pyplot.scatter()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Nothing, just plots the figure and optionally saves the plot.

signature: squidpy.gr.nhood_enrichment(adata, cluster_key, library_key=None, connectivity_key=None, n_perms=1000, numba_parallel=False, seed=None, copy=False, n_jobs=None, backend='loky', show_progress_bar=True)
description: Compute neighborhood enrichment by permutation test.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: library_key
params.desc: If multiple library_id, column in `anndata.AnnData.obs` which stores mapping between `library_id` and obs.

params.name: connectivity_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obsp` where spatial connectivities are stored. Default is: `['spatial_connectivities']`.

params.name: n_perms
params.desc: Number of permutations for the permutation test.

params.name: numba_parallel
params.desc: Whether to use `numba.prange` or not. If None, it is determined automatically. For small datasets or small number of interactions, it’s recommended to set this to False.

params.name: seed
params.desc: Random seed for reproducibility.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the `adata` object.

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs.

params.name: backend
params.desc: Parallelization backend to use.

params.name: show_progress_bar
params.desc: Whether to show the progress bar or not.

params.name: return
params.desc:
If `copy = True`, returns a tuple with the z-score and the enrichment count.

Otherwise, modifies the `adata` with the following keys:
- `anndata.AnnData.uns['{cluster_key}_nhood_enrichment']['zscore']` - the enrichment z-score.
- `anndata.AnnData.uns['{cluster_key}_nhood_enrichment']['count']` - the enrichment count.

signature: squidpy.pl.centrality_scores(adata, cluster_key, score=None, legend_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), palette=None, figsize=None, dpi=None, save=None, **kwargs)
description: Plot centrality scores.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: score
params.desc: Whether to plot all scores or only selected ones.

params.name: legend_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.pyplot.legend()`.

params.name: palette
params.desc: Categorical colormap for the clusters. If `None`, use `anndata.AnnData.uns` `['{cluster_key}_colors']`, if available.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: dpi
params.desc: Dots per inch.

params.name: save
params.desc: Whether to save the plot.

params.name: scalebar_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib_scalebar.ScaleBar()`.

params.name: edges_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `networkx.draw_networkx_edges()`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.pyplot.scatter()` or `matplotlib.pyplot.imshow()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Nothing, just plots the figure and optionally saves the plot.

misc: The centrality scores are computed by `squidpy.gr.centrality_scores()`.

signature: squidpy.datasets.seqfish(path=None, **kwargs)
description: Pre-processed subset seqFISH dataset from Lohoff et al. The shape of this anndata.AnnData object (19416, 351).

params.name: path
params.desc: Path where to save the dataset.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for scanpy.read().

params.name: return
params.desc: The dataset.

signature: squidpy.gr.spatial_autocorr(adata, connectivity_key='spatial_connectivities', genes=None, mode='moran', transformation=True, n_perms=None, two_tailed=False, corr_method='fdr_bh', attr='X', layer=None, seed=None, use_raw=False, copy=False, n_jobs=None, backend='loky', show_progress_bar=True)
description: Calculate Global Autocorrelation Statistic (Moran’s I or Geary’s C).

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: connectivity_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsp where spatial connectivities are stored. Default is: anndata.AnnData.obsp ['spatial_connectivities'].

params.name: genes
params.desc:
Depending on the attr:
> - if `attr = 'X'`, it corresponds to genes stored in anndata.AnnData.var_names.
>   If None, it’s computed anndata.AnnData.var ['highly_variable'],
>   if present. Otherwise, it’s computed for all genes.
> - if `attr = 'obs'`, it corresponds to a list of columns in anndata.AnnData.obs.
>   If None, use all numerical columns.
> - if `attr = 'obsm'`, it corresponds to indices in anndata.AnnData.obsm ['{layer}'].
>   If None, all indices are used.

params.name: mode
params.desc:
Mode of score calculation:
> - ’moran’ - Moran’s I autocorrelation.
> - ’geary’ - Geary’s C autocorrelation.

params.name: transformation
params.desc: If True, weights in anndata.AnnData.obsp ['spatial_connectivities'] are row-normalized, advised for analytic p-value calculation.

params.name: n_perms
params.desc: Number of permutations for the permutation test. If None, only p-values under normality assumption are computed.

params.name: two_tailed
params.desc: If True, p-values are two-tailed, otherwise they are one-tailed.

params.name: corr_method
params.desc: Correction method for multiple testing. See statsmodels.stats.multitest.multipletests() for valid options.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Whether to access anndata.AnnData.raw. Only used when `attr = 'X'`.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Depending on `attr`: Layer in anndata.AnnData.layers to use. If None, use anndata.AnnData.X.

params.name: attr
params.desc: Which attribute of AnnData to access. See `genes` parameter for more information.

params.name: seed
params.desc: Random seed for reproducibility.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the `adata` object.

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs.

params.name: backend
params.desc: Parallelization backend to use. See joblib.Parallel for available options.

params.name: show_progress_bar
params.desc: Whether to show the progress bar or not.

params.name: return
params.desc:
If `copy = True`, returns a pandas.DataFrame with the following keys:
> - ’I’ or ‘C’ - Moran’s I or Geary’s C statistic.
> - ’pval_norm’ - p-value under normality assumption.
> - ’var_norm’ - variance of ‘score’ under normality assumption.
> - ’{p_val}_{corr_method}’ - the corrected p-values if `corr_method != None` .

If `n_perms != None`, additionally returns the following columns:
> - ’pval_z_sim’ - p-value based on standard normal approximation from permutations.
> - ’pval_sim’ - p-value based on permutations.
> - ’var_sim’ - variance of ‘score’ from permutations.

Otherwise, modifies the `adata` with the following key:
> - anndata.AnnData.uns ['moranI'] - the above mentioned dataframe, if `mode = 'moran'`.
> - anndata.AnnData.uns ['gearyC'] - the above mentioned dataframe, if `mode = 'geary'`.

misc: See Rey and Anselin, 2010 for reference.

signature: squidpy.im.process(img, layer=None, library_id=None, method='smooth', chunks=None, lazy=False, layer_added=None, channel_dim=None, copy=False, apply_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), **kwargs)
description: Process an image by applying a transformation.

params.name: img
params.desc: High-resolution image.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Image layer in `img` that should be processed. If None and only 1 layer is present, it will be selected.

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Name of the Z-dimension(s) that this function should be applied to. For not specified Z-dimensions, the identity function is applied. If None, all Z-dimensions are processed at once, treating the image as a 3D volume.

params.name: method
params.desc:
Processing method to use. Valid options are:
> - ’smooth’ - `skimage.filters.gaussian()`.
> - ’gray’ - `skimage.color.rgb2gray()`.
Alternatively, any `callable()` can be passed as long as it has the following signature:
`numpy.ndarray (height, width, channels) -> numpy.ndarray (height, width[, channels])`.

params.name: chunks
params.desc: Number of chunks for `dask`. For automatic chunking, use `chunks = 'auto'`.

params.name: lazy
params.desc: Whether to lazily compute the result or not. Only used when `chunks != None`.

params.name: layer_added
params.desc: Layer of new image layer to add into `img` object. If None, use `'{layer}_{method}'`.

params.name: channel_dim
params.desc: Name of the channel dimension of the new image layer. Default is the same as the original, if the processing function does not change the number of channels, and `'{channel}_{processing}'` otherwise.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the image container.

params.name: apply_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `squidpy.im.ImageContainer.apply()`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `method`.

params.name: return
params.desc:
If `copy = True`, returns a new container with the processed image in `'{layer_added}'.

Otherwise, modifies the `img` with the following key:
> - `squidpy.im.ImageContainer ['{layer_added}']` - the processed image.

misc: Raises: **NotImplementedError** – If `method` has not been implemented.

signature: squidpy.gr.co_occurrence(adata, cluster_key, spatial_key='spatial', interval=50, copy=False, n_splits=None, n_jobs=None, backend='loky', show_progress_bar=True)
description: Compute co-occurrence probability of clusters.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obsm` where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: interval
params.desc: Distances interval at which co-occurrence is computed. If `int`, uniformly spaced interval of the given size will be used.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the `adata` object.

params.name: n_splits
params.desc: Number of splits in which to divide the spatial coordinates in `anndata.AnnData.obsm` `['{spatial_key}']`.

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs.

params.name: backend
params.desc: Parallelization backend to use.

params.name: show_progress_bar
params.desc: Whether to show the progress bar or not.

params.name: return
params.desc:
If `copy = True`, returns the co-occurrence probability and the distance thresholds intervals.

  Otherwise, modifies the `adata` with the following keys: - `anndata.AnnData.uns['{cluster_key}_co_occurrence']['occ']` - the co-occurrence probabilities across interval thresholds. - `anndata.AnnData.uns['{cluster_key}_co_occurrence']['interval']` - the distance thresholds computed at `interval`.

signature: squidpy.datasets.visium_fluo_image_crop(path=None, **kwargs)
description: Cropped Fluorescent image from 10x Genomics Visium dataset. The shape of this image is `(7272, 7272)`.

params.name: path
params.desc: Path where to save the .tiff image.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `squidpy.im.ImageContainer.add_img()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: The image data.

signature: squidpy.gr.spatial_neighbors(adata, spatial_key='spatial', elements_to_coordinate_systems=None, table_key=None, library_key=None, coord_type=None, n_neighs=6, radius=None, delaunay=False, n_rings=1, percentile=None, transform=None, set_diag=False, key_added='spatial', copy=False)
description: Create a graph from spatial coordinates.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsm where spatial coordinates are stored. If adata is a spatialdata.SpatialData, the coordinates of the centroids will be stored in the adata with this key.

params.name: elements_to_coordinate_systems
params.desc: A dictionary mapping element names of the SpatialData object to coordinate systems. The elements can be either Shapes or Labels. For compatibility, the spatialdata table must annotate all regions keys. Must not be None if adata is a spatialdata.SpatialData.

params.name: table_key
params.desc: Key in spatialdata.SpatialData.tables where the spatialdata table is stored. Must not be None if adata is a spatialdata.SpatialData.

params.name: mask_polygon
params.desc: The Polygon or MultiPolygon element.

params.name: library_key
params.desc: If multiple library_id, column in anndata.AnnData.obs which stores mapping between library_id and obs.

params.name: coord_type
params.desc:
Type of coordinate system. Valid options are:
- 'grid' - grid coordinates.
- 'generic' - generic coordinates.
- None - 'grid' if spatial_key is in anndata.AnnData.uns with n_neighs = 6 (Visium), otherwise use 'generic'.

params.name: n_neighs
params.desc:
Depending on the coord_type:
- 'grid' - number of neighboring tiles.
- 'generic' - number of neighborhoods for non-grid data. Only used when delaunay = False.

params.name: radius
params.desc:
Only available when coord_type = 'generic'. Depending on the type:
- float - compute the graph based on neighborhood radius.
- tuple - prune the final graph to only contain edges in interval [min(radius), max(radius)].

params.name: delaunay
params.desc: Whether to compute the graph from Delaunay triangulation. Only used when coord_type = 'generic'.

params.name: n_rings
params.desc: Number of rings of neighbors for grid data. Only used when coord_type = 'grid'.

params.name: percentile
params.desc: Percentile of the distances to use as threshold. Only used when coord_type = 'generic'.

params.name: transform
params.desc:
Type of adjacency matrix transform. Valid options are:
- 'spectral' - spectral transformation of the adjacency matrix.
- 'cosine' - cosine transformation of the adjacency matrix.
- None - no transformation of the adjacency matrix.

params.name: set_diag
params.desc: Whether to set the diagonal of the spatial connectivities to 1.0.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key which controls where the results are saved if copy = False.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If True, return the result, otherwise save it to the adata object.

params.name: return
params.desc:
If copy = True, returns a tuple with the spatial connectivities and distances matrices. Otherwise, modifies the adata with the following keys:
- anndata.AnnData.obsp ['{key_added}_connectivities'] - the spatial connectivities.
- anndata.AnnData.obsp ['{key_added}_distances'] - the spatial distances.
- anndata.AnnData.uns ['{key_added}'] - dict containing parameters.

signature: squidpy.pl.spatial_scatter(adata, shape=None, color=None, groups=None, library_id=None, library_key=None, spatial_key='spatial', img=True, img_res_key='hires', img_alpha=None, img_cmap=None, img_channel=None, use_raw=None, layer=None, alt_var=None, size=None, size_key='spot_diameter_fullres', scale_factor=None, crop_coord=None, cmap=None, palette=None, alpha=1.0, norm=None, na_color=(0, 0, 0, 0), connectivity_key=None, edges_width=1.0, edges_color='grey', library_first=True, frameon=None, wspace=None, hspace=0.25, ncols=4, outline=False, outline_color=('black', 'white'), outline_width=(0.3, 0.05), legend_loc='right margin', legend_fontsize=None, legend_fontweight='bold', legend_fontoutline=None, legend_na=True, colorbar=True, scalebar_dx=None, scalebar_units=None, title=None, axis_label=None, fig=None, ax=None, return_ax=False, figsize=None, dpi=None, save=None, scalebar_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), edges_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), **kwargs)
description: Plot spatial omics data with data overlayed on top. The plotted shapes (circles, squares or hexagons) have a real “size” with respect to their coordinate space, which can be specified via the `size` or `size_key` argument.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: shape
params.desc: Whether to plot scatter plot of points or regular polygons.

params.name: color
params.desc: Key for annotations in `anndata.AnnData.obs` or variables/genes.

params.name: groups
params.desc: For discrete annotation in `color`, select which values to plot (other values are set to NAs).

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Name of the Z-dimension(s) that this function should be applied to.

params.name: library_key
params.desc: If multiple library_id, column in `anndata.AnnData.obs` which stores mapping between `library_id` and obs.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obsm` where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: img
params.type: bool | ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[Any]] | Sequence[ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[Any]]] | None
params.desc: Whether to plot the image. One (or more) `numpy.ndarray` can also be passed for plotting.

params.name: img_res_key
params.desc: Key for image resolution, used to get `img` and `scale_factor` from `'images'` and `'scalefactors'` entries for this library.

params.name: img_alpha
params.desc: Alpha value for the underlying image.

params.name: img_cmap
params.desc: Colormap for the image, see `matplotlib.colors.Colormap`.

params.name: img_channel
params.desc: To select which channel to plot (all by default).

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: If True, use `anndata.AnnData.raw`.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.layers` or None for `anndata.AnnData.X`.

params.name: alt_var
params.desc: Which column to use in `anndata.AnnData.var` to select alternative `var_name`.

params.name: size
params.desc: Size of the scatter point/shape. In case of `spatial_shape` it represents to the scaling factor for shape (accessed via `size_key`). In case of `spatial_point`, it represents the `size` argument in `matplotlib.pyplot.scatter()`.

params.name: size_key
params.desc: Key of of pixel size of shapes to be plotted, stored in `anndata.AnnData.uns`.

params.name: scale_factor
params.desc: Scaling factor used to map from coordinate space to pixel space.

params.name: crop_coord
params.type: tuple[int, int, int, int] | Sequence[tuple[int, int, int, int]] | None
params.desc: Coordinates to use for cropping the image (left, right, top, bottom).

params.name: cmap
params.desc: Colormap for continuous annotations, see `matplotlib.colors.Colormap`.

params.name: palette
params.desc: Palette for discrete annotations, see `matplotlib.colors.Colormap`.

params.name: alpha
params.desc: Alpha value for scatter point/shape.

params.name: norm
params.desc: Colormap normalization for continuous annotations, see `matplotlib.colors.Normalize`.

params.name: na_color
params.desc: Color to be used for NAs values, if present.

params.name: connectivity_key
params.desc: Key for neighbors graph to plot. Default is: `anndata.AnnData.obsp` `['spatial_connectivities']`.

params.name: edges_width
params.desc: Width of the edges. Only used when `connectivity_key != None` .

params.name: edges_color
params.desc: Color of the edges.

params.name: library_first
params.desc: If multiple libraries are plotted, set the plotting order with respect to `color`.

params.name: frameon
params.desc: If True, draw a frame around the panels.

params.name: wspace
params.desc: Width space between panels.

params.name: hspace
params.desc: Height space between panels.

params.name: ncols
params.desc: Number of panels per row.

params.name: outline
params.desc: If True, a thin border around points/shapes is plotted.

params.name: outline_color
params.desc: Color of the border.

params.name: outline_width
params.desc: Width of the border.

params.name: legend_loc
params.desc: Location of the legend, see `matplotlib.legend.Legend`.

params.name: legend_fontsize
params.desc: Font size of the legend, see `matplotlib.text.Text.set_fontsize()`.

params.name: legend_fontweight
params.desc: Font weight of the legend, see `matplotlib.text.Text.set_fontweight()`.

params.name: legend_fontoutline
params.desc: Font outline of the legend, see `matplotlib.patheffects.withStroke`.

params.name: legend_na
params.desc: Whether to show NA values in the legend.

params.name: colorbar
params.desc: Whether to show the colorbar, see `matplotlib.pyplot.colorbar()`.

params.name: scalebar_dx
params.desc: Size of one pixel in units specified by `scalebar_units`.

params.name: scalebar_units
params.desc: Units of `scalebar_dx`.

params.name: title
params.desc: Panel titles.

params.name: axis_label
params.desc: Panel axis labels.

params.name: fig
params.desc: Optional `matplotlib.figure.Figure` to use.

params.name: ax
params.desc: Optional `matplotlib.axes.Axes` to use.

params.name: return_ax
params.desc: Whether to return `matplotlib.axes.Axes` object(s).

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: dpi
params.desc: Dots per inch.

params.name: save
params.desc: Whether to save the plot.

params.name: scalebar_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib_scalebar.ScaleBar()`.

params.name: edges_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `networkx.draw_networkx_edges()`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.pyplot.scatter()` or `matplotlib.pyplot.imshow()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Nothing, just plots the figure and optionally saves the plot.

misc:
#### SEE ALSO
- `squidpy.pl.spatial_segment()` on how to plot spatial data with segmentation masks on top.

signature: squidpy.pl.nhood_enrichment(adata, cluster_key, mode='zscore', annotate=False, method=None, title=None, cmap='viridis', palette=None, cbar_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), figsize=None, dpi=None, save=None, ax=None, **kwargs)
description: Plot neighborhood enrichment. The enrichment is computed by `squidpy.gr.nhood_enrichment()`.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: mode
params.desc:
Which `squidpy.gr.nhood_enrichment()` result to plot. Valid options are:
> - ’zscore’ - z-score values of enrichment statistic.
> - ’count’ - enrichment count.

params.name: annotate
params.desc: Whether to annotate the cells of the heatmap.

params.name: method
params.desc: The linkage method to be used for dendrogram/clustering, see `scipy.cluster.hierarchy.linkage()`.

params.name: title
params.desc: The title of the plot.

params.name: cmap
params.desc: Continuous colormap to use.

params.name: palette
params.desc: Categorical colormap for the clusters. If None, use `anndata.AnnData.uns['{cluster_key}_colors']`, if available.

params.name: cbar_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.figure.Figure.colorbar()`.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: dpi
params.desc: Dots per inch.

params.name: save
params.desc: Whether to save the plot.

params.name: ax
params.desc: Axes, `matplotlib.axes.Axes`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.pyplot.text()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Nothing, just plots the figure and optionally saves the plot.

signature: squidpy.gr.ripley(adata, cluster_key, mode='F', spatial_key='spatial', metric='euclidean', n_neigh=2, n_simulations=100, n_observations=1000, max_dist=None, n_steps=50, seed=None, copy=False)
description: Calculate various Ripley's statistics for point processes.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obs where clustering is stored.

params.name: mode
params.desc: Which Ripley’s statistic to compute.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsm where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: metric
params.desc: Which metric to use for computing distances. For available metrics, check out sklearn.neighbors.DistanceMetric.

params.name: n_neigh
params.desc: Number of neighbors to consider for the KNN graph.

params.name: n_simulations
params.desc: How many simulations to run for computing p-values.

params.name: n_observations
params.desc: How many observations to generate for the Spatial Poisson Point Process.

params.name: max_dist
params.desc: Maximum distances for the support. If None, max_dist=\(\sqrt{area \over 2}\).

params.name: n_steps
params.desc: Number of steps for the support.

params.name: seed
params.desc: Random seed for reproducibility.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If True, return the result, otherwise save it to the adata object.

params.name: return
params.desc:
If copy = True, returns a dict with keys:
- ‘{mode}_stat’ - DataFrame containing the statistics of choice for the real observations.
- ‘sims_stat’ - DataFrame containing the statistics of choice for the simulations.
- ‘bins’ - ndarray containing the support.
- ‘pvalues’ - ndarray containing the p-values for the statistics of interest.
Otherwise, modifies the adata object with the following key:
- anndata.AnnData.uns ['{key_added}'] - the above mentioned dict.
Statistics and p-values are computed for each cluster anndata.AnnData.obs ['{cluster_key}'] separately.

misc: For reference, Ripley's statistics can be found on Wikipedia or in the paper by Baddeley et al., 2015.

signature: squidpy.pl.interaction_matrix(adata, cluster_key, annotate=False, method=None, title=None, cmap='viridis', palette=None, cbar_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), figsize=None, dpi=None, save=None, ax=None, **kwargs)
description: Plot cluster interaction matrix.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: annotate
params.desc: Whether to annotate the cells of the heatmap.

params.name: method
params.desc: The linkage method to be used for dendrogram/clustering.

params.name: title
params.desc: The title of the plot.

params.name: cmap
params.desc: Continuous colormap to use.

params.name: cbar_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.figure.Figure.colorbar()`.

params.name: palette
params.desc: Categorical colormap for the clusters. If None, use `anndata.AnnData.uns['{cluster_key}_colors']`, if available.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: dpi
params.desc: Dots per inch.

params.name: save
params.desc: Whether to save the plot.

params.name: ax
params.desc: Axes, `matplotlib.axes.Axes`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.pyplot.text()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Nothing, just plots the figure and optionally saves the plot.

misc: The interaction matrix is computed by `squidpy.gr.interaction_matrix()`.

signature: squidpy.read.visium(path, *, counts_file='filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5', library_id=None, load_images=True, source_image_path=None, **kwargs)
description: Read *10x Genomics* Visium formatted dataset. In addition to reading the regular *Visium* output, it looks for the *spatial* directory and loads the images, spatial coordinates and scale factors.

params.name: path
params.desc: Path to the root directory containing *Visium* files.

params.name: counts_file
params.desc: Which file in the passed directory to use as the count file. Typically either *filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5* or *raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5*.

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Identifier for the *Visium* library. Useful when concatenating multiple anndata.AnnData objects.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for scanpy.read_10x_h5(), anndata.read_mtx() or read_text().

params.name: return
params.desc: Annotated data object with the following keys: anndata.AnnData.obsm ['spatial'] - spatial spot coordinates; anndata.AnnData.uns ['spatial']['{library_id}']['images'] - *hires* and *lowres* images; anndata.AnnData.uns ['spatial']['{library_id}']['scalefactors'] - scale factors for the spots; anndata.AnnData.uns ['spatial']['{library_id}']['metadata'] - various metadata.

misc:
SEE ALSO
- Space Ranger output
- squidpy.pl.spatial_scatter() on how to plot spatial data.

signature: squidpy.gr.ligrec(adata, cluster_key, interactions=None, complex_policy='min', threshold=0.01, corr_method=None, corr_axis='clusters', use_raw=True, copy=False, key_added=None, gene_symbols=None, **kwargs)
description: Perform the permutation test as described in Efremova et al., 2020.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: interactions
params.type: DataFrame | Mapping[str, Sequence[str]] | Sequence[str] | tuple[Sequence[str], Sequence[str]] | Sequence[tuple[str, str]] | None
params.desc: Interaction to test. The type can be one of these specified formats. If None, the interactions are extracted from `omnipath`.

params.name: complex_policy
params.desc: Policy on how to handle complexes. Valid options are: 'min' for selecting gene with minimum average expression; 'all' for selecting all possible combinations between 'source' and 'target' complexes.

params.name: threshold
params.desc: Do not perform permutation test if any of the interacting components is being expressed in less than `threshold` percent of cells within a given cluster.

params.name: corr_method
params.desc: Correction method for multiple testing.

params.name: corr_axis
params.desc: Axis over which to perform the FDR correction. Only used when `corr_method != None`.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Whether to access `anndata.AnnData.raw`.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the `adata` object.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.uns` where the result is stored if `copy = False`. If None, `'{cluster_key}_ligrec'` will be used.

params.name: gene_symbols
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.var` to use instead of `anndata.AnnData.var_names`.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `copy = True`, returns a dict with following keys: 'means', 'pvalues', and 'metadata'. Otherwise, modifies the `adata` object with the key: `anndata.AnnData.uns` ['{key_added}'].

misc: NaN p-values mark combinations for which the mean expression of one of the interacting components was 0 or it didn’t pass the `threshold` percentage of cells being expressed within a given cluster.

signature: squidpy.read.nanostring(path, *, counts_file, meta_file, fov_file=None)
description:
Read *Nanostring* formatted dataset.

In addition to reading the regular *Nanostring* output, it loads the metadata file, if present *CellComposite* and *CellLabels* directories containing the images and optionally the field of view file.

params.name: path
params.desc: Path to the root directory containing *Nanostring* files.

params.name: counts_file
params.desc: File containing the counts. Typically ends with  *_exprMat_file.csv*.

params.name: meta_file
params.desc: File containing the spatial coordinates and additional cell-level metadata. Typically ends with  *_metadata_file.csv*.

params.name: fov_file
params.desc: File containing the coordinates of all the fields of view.

params.name: return
params.desc:
Annotated data object with the following keys:
- `AnnData.obsm['spatial']` - local coordinates of the centers of cells.
- `AnnData.obsm['spatial_fov']` - global coordinates of the centers of cells in the field of view.
- `AnnData.uns['spatial']['{fov}']['images']` - *hires* and *segmentation* images.
- `AnnData.uns['spatial']['{fov}']['metadata']]['{x,y}_global_px']` - coordinates of the field of view. Only present if `fov_file != None`.

misc:
SEE ALSO
- Nanostring Spatial Molecular Imager.
- `squidpy.pl.spatial_scatter()` on how to plot spatial data.

description: Pre-processed 10x Genomics Visium H&E dataset.

params.name: path
params.desc: Path where to save the dataset.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for scanpy.read().

params.name: return
params.desc: The dataset.

misc: The shape of this anndata.AnnData object (2688, 18078).

signature: squidpy.gr.sepal(adata, max_neighs, genes=None, n_iter=30000, dt=0.001, thresh=1e-08, connectivity_key='spatial_connectivities', spatial_key='spatial', layer=None, use_raw=False, copy=False, n_jobs=None, backend='loky', show_progress_bar=True)
description: Identify spatially variable genes with Sepal. Sepal is a method that simulates a diffusion process to quantify spatial structure in tissue.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: max_neighs
params.desc: Maximum number of neighbors of a node in the graph. Valid options are: 4 - for a square-grid (ST, Dbit-seq). 6 - for a hexagonal-grid (Visium).

params.name: genes
params.desc: List of gene names, used to compute sepal score. If None, it’s computed based on available data.

params.name: n_iter
params.desc: Maximum number of iterations for the diffusion simulation. If n_iter iterations are reached, the simulation will terminate even though convergence has not been achieved.

params.name: dt
params.desc: Time step in diffusion simulation.

params.name: thresh
params.desc: Entropy threshold for convergence of diffusion simulation.

params.name: connectivity_key
params.desc: Key where spatial connectivities are stored. Default is 'spatial_connectivities'.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Layer to use. If None, use default data.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Whether to access raw data.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If True, return the result, otherwise save it to the adata object.

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs.

params.name: backend
params.desc: Parallelization backend to use.

params.name: show_progress_bar
params.desc: Whether to show the progress bar or not.

params.name: return
params.desc: If copy = True, returns a DataFrame with the sepal scores. Otherwise, modifies the adata with the sepal scores.

misc: If some genes in `anndata.AnnData.uns['sepal_score']` are NaN, consider re-running the function with increased `n_iter`.

signature: squidpy.datasets.visium(sample_id, *, include_hires_tiff=False, base_dir=None)
description: Download Visium datasets from 10x Genomics.

params.name: sample_id
params.desc: Name of the Visium dataset.

params.name: include_hires_tiff
params.desc: Whether to download the high-resolution tissue section into `anndata.AnnData.uns ['spatial']['{sample_id}']['metadata']['source_image_path']`.

params.name: base_dir
params.desc: Directory where to download the data. If None, use `scanpy._settings.ScanpyConfig.datasetdir`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Spatial `anndata.AnnData`.

signature: squidpy.gr.interaction_matrix(adata, cluster_key, connectivity_key=None, normalized=False, copy=False, weights=False)
description: Compute interaction matrix for clusters.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obs where clustering is stored.

params.name: connectivity_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsp where spatial connectivities are stored. Default is: anndata.AnnData.obsp['spatial_connectivities'].

params.name: normalized
params.desc: If True, each row is normalized to sum to 1.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If True, return the result, otherwise save it to the adata object.

params.name: weights
params.desc: Whether to use edge weights or binarize.

params.name: return
params.desc: If copy = True, returns the interaction matrix. Otherwise, modifies the adata with the following key: - anndata.AnnData.uns['{cluster_key}_interactions'] - the interaction matrix.

signature: squidpy.datasets.mibitof(path=None, **kwargs)

params.name: path
params.desc: Path where to save the dataset.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for scanpy.read().

params.name: return
params.desc: The dataset.

signature: squidpy.pl.extract(adata, obsm_key='img_features', prefix=None)
description: Create a temporary AnnData object for plotting by moving columns from AnnData.obsm to AnnData.obs to enable the use of scanpy.plotting functions.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: obsm_key
params.desc: Entries in AnnData.obsm that should be moved to AnnData.obs.

params.name: prefix
params.desc: Prefix to prepend to each column name. Should be a list if obsm_key is a list. If None, use the original column names.

params.name: return
params.desc: Temporary AnnData object with desired entries in AnnData.obs.

misc: Raises ValueError if number of prefixes does not fit to number of obsm_keys. If AnnData.obs['{column}'] already exists, it will be overwritten and a warning will be issued.

params.name: return
params.desc: The dataset.

misc: The shape of this `anndata.AnnData` object is `(2800, 16562)`.

signature: squidpy.pl.co_occurrence(adata, cluster_key, palette=None, clusters=None, figsize=None, dpi=None, save=None, legend_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), **kwargs)
description: Plot co-occurrence probability ratio for each cluster.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: clusters
params.desc: Cluster instances for which to plot conditional probability.

params.name: palette
params.desc: Categorical colormap for the clusters. If `None`, use `anndata.AnnData.uns['{cluster_key}_colors']`, if available.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: dpi
params.desc: Dots per inch.

params.name: save
params.desc: Whether to save the plot.

params.name: scalebar_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib_scalebar.ScaleBar()`.

params.name: edges_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `networkx.draw_networkx_edges()`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.pyplot.scatter()` or `matplotlib.pyplot.imshow()`.

params.name: legend_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `matplotlib.pyplot.legend()`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `seaborn.lineplot()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Nothing, just plots the figure and optionally saves the plot.

misc: The co-occurrence is computed by `squidpy.gr.co_occurrence()`.
